parameters.text:
=Clean this landing page markdown by following these rules:

REMOVE:
- Navigation menus, headers, footers
- Cookie notices, privacy banners, popups
- Social media links, share buttons
- Tracking scripts, analytics code
- Breadcrumbs, pagination
- "Skip to content" links
- Metadata tags (title, description, og:image, etc.)
- Generic footer text (copyright, terms, privacy policy links)

KEEP:
- All headlines and subheadlines
- Body copy and paragraphs
- Bullet points and lists
- CTAs (call-to-action buttons and links)
- Pricing information, offers, discounts
- Testimonials, reviews, social proof
- Product benefits and features
- Guarantees, trust badges (text only)
- FAQs
- Any content a visitor would read while scrolling

PRESERVE:
- Original markdown formatting (headings, lists, bold, italic)
- Logical content flow and structure

---

{{ $json.data.markdown }}

options.systemMessage:
=You are a content extraction specialist for landing page analysis. Your job is to clean markdown content by removing navigational elements, tracking code, and boilerplate while preserving all marketing copy that a visitor would actually read.

Output ONLY the cleaned markdown with no explanations, commentary, or meta-text.

parameters.text:
=Extract reusable marketing elements from this native advertising landing page across 5 standardized categories.
These elements will be presented to users as selectable options when building new advertorials. Write each item as a direct statement or claim that can be reused, not as an analytical observation.

INPUT SOURCES:
- MARKDOWN (cleaned text content below)
- SCREENSHOT (provided as image)

---

USER CONTEXT (if provided):
{{ $('Loop Over Items').item.json['body.urls'].description }}

If the user has provided context above, use it to:
- Understand what type of page this is (product page, competitor advertorial, landing page, etc.)
- Focus extraction on the areas they've highlighted as important
- Prioritize elements relevant to their stated goals
- Ignore sections they've indicated are not relevant

If no context is provided, extract comprehensively across all categories.

---

MARKDOWN CONTENT:
{{ $json.output }}

---

SCREENSHOT URL:
{{ $('Scrape').item.json.data.screenshot }}

---

EXTRACTION REQUIREMENTS:

Extract elements for these 5 categories:

**1. USPs (Unique Selling Propositions)**
Extract 4-6 benefit statements, value propositions, or selling points exactly as they appear. These should be:
- Direct benefit claims (e.g., "Plans from just £7.50 per month")
- Specific guarantees or promises
- Social proof statements (e.g., "Trusted by 500,000+ families")
- Trust indicators (e.g., "No health questionnaires required")
- Risk reversals (e.g., "Money-back guarantee within 30 days")

Write each as a standalone claim that could be used in any advertorial.

**2. PRICING**
Extract pricing statements exactly as presented. Include:
- Price points with full context (e.g., "Standard Plan £12.99/month (was £19.99)")
- Payment options or tiers
- Discount offers
- Pricing qualifiers (e.g., "introductory rate", "covers up to £500/year")

If no pricing found, write: "No pricing information found"

**3. MAIN ANGLE (Messaging/Positioning)**
Extract 2-4 messaging approaches as directive statements:
- Target audience positioning (e.g., "Appeals to UK adults 30-60 worried about dental costs")
- Problem-solution framing (e.g., "Addresses NHS dentist shortage by offering immediate private coverage")
- Emotional triggers (e.g., "Creates urgency around avoiding unexpected emergency bills")
- Unique positioning (e.g., "Positions as affordable alternative to expensive private dentistry")

Write each as a messaging directive, not an observation.

**4. TONE OF VOICE**
Extract 2-3 tone characteristics as style directives with examples:
- Writing style to emulate
- Emotional approach
- Language patterns to use

Format: "[Tone directive] - Example: '[exact quote showing this tone]'"

**5. KEY HOOKS (Headlines & CTAs to Adapt)**
Extract 3-5 hooks as templates or examples:
- Headlines that work
- Subheadlines
- Opening teasers
- CTA phrasing
- Curiosity drivers

Format: "[Hook type]: '[exact text]'"

---

INSTRUCTIONS:
- Write as if suggesting "Include this" or "Use this approach" - NOT "This page does X"
- Extract exact text for USPs, pricing, and hooks
- Make tone and angle items actionable directives
- Each item should stand alone - no analytical framing
- Prioritize SCREENSHOT for headlines, prices, visual CTAs
- Prioritize MARKDOWN for body copy, benefits, details
- If user context emphasizes specific areas, weight extraction toward those areas

---

OUTPUT FORMAT:

=== USPs ===
- [Direct benefit claim or selling point]
- [Another benefit claim]
- [Continue for all USPs]

=== PRICING ===
- [Pricing statement with full context]
- [Another pricing statement]
- [Continue for all pricing found]

=== MAIN ANGLE ===
- [Messaging approach directive]
- [Another angle directive]
- [Continue for all angles]

=== TONE OF VOICE ===
- [Tone to emulate] - Example: "[exact quote demonstrating this]"
- [Another tone directive] - Example: "[exact quote]"
- [Continue for all tone characteristics]

=== KEY HOOKS ===
- [Hook type]: "[exact text to adapt or emulate]"
- [Hook type]: "[exact text]"
- [Continue for all hooks]

Do not add any preamble, commentary, or closing remarks. Start directly with "=== USPs ===" and end after the last hook.

options.systemMessage:
=You are a native advertising copywriting analyst extracting reusable marketing elements from high-performing landing pages.

You will receive:
1. Cleaned markdown content (text-based)
2. Screenshot of the page (visual-based)

Your task is to extract marketing elements as direct, reusable statements that can be selected and applied to new advertorials. These are NOT analytical observations - they are building blocks.

Extract what's working on this page in a format that can be directly reused. Write statements as if they are suggestions for what to include in a new campaign.

Cross-reference both sources. Visual elements take priority for headlines and CTAs.

Output your extraction in a clean, structured format with clear section headers.

parameters.text:
=Consolidate these reference page analyses into a single, curated set of insights.

You will receive results from multiple scraped pages. Your job is to create one final, high-quality list by:
- Selecting the best items across all pages
- Merging similar or duplicate items (keep the best-worded version)
- Removing redundant or low-quality items
- Ensuring variety and actionability

---

**INPUT DATA:**
{{ $json.formattedData }}

---

**CONSOLIDATION TARGETS:**

**1. USPs - Up to 10 items**
- Prioritize specific, quantified claims (e.g., "Reduces shedding by 77%" over "Reduces shedding")
- Include diverse types: pricing benefits, guarantees, social proof, features, specific results
- Merge duplicates (e.g., if multiple pages mention money-back guarantee, keep one best version)
- Prefer concrete numbers, timeframes, and specific outcomes
- Look for: "X% reduction", "in Y weeks", "by Z customers", "without [negative]"

**2. PRICING - Up to 5 items**
- Include diverse pricing options (different tiers, packages, offers)
- Prioritize complete pricing info (price + context + discount if applicable)
- If multiple pages show similar pricing, consolidate into clearest version
- Keep discount percentages and "was/now" pricing when available
- Include payment frequency (per month, one-time, etc.)
- Return fewer items if not enough unique pricing exists

**3. MAIN ANGLE - Up to 3 items**
- Choose the most distinct positioning approaches
- Avoid overlapping angles (e.g., don't pick 2 variations of "fear of aging")
- Each angle should clearly define: target audience + emotional trigger + unique approach
- Prioritize angles that explain WHY the positioning works
- Look for: problem-solution framing, urgency tactics, authority positioning, unique mechanisms

**4. TONE OF VOICE - Up to 3 items**
- Choose distinct tone characteristics with compelling example quotes
- Ensure variety (e.g., don't pick 3 variations of "urgent")
- Prioritize tones with quotable examples that clearly demonstrate the characteristic
- Keep the exact example quotes that best show each tone
- Look for: emotional approach, language patterns, formality level, perspective used

**5. KEY HOOKS - Up to 5 items**
- Include variety: at least 1 headline, 1 CTA, and mix of others
- Prioritize hooks with specific numbers, curiosity gaps, or strong emotional triggers
- Avoid redundant hooks (e.g., multiple CTAs saying same thing)
- Choose hooks that could work across different products/verticals
- Keep hook type labels (Headline, CTA, Teaser, etc.) and exact text in quotes

---

**SELECTION CRITERIA:**

**Prefer items with:**
- Specific data points and numbers
- Timeframes and quantified results
- Strong emotional triggers
- Clear target audience definition
- Proven social proof (specific user counts)
- Urgency and scarcity elements
- Risk reversals and guarantees

**Avoid items with:**
- Vague or generic statements
- Overly wordy phrasing
- Duplicate information across categories
- Lack of context or specifics
- Generic "high quality" claims without proof

---

**MERGING GUIDELINES:**

When you find similar items across multiple pages:

1. Compare quality and specificity
2. Choose the version with more concrete details
3. If both are good but different, combine the best elements into one item

**Example merging:**
- Page 1: "90-day money-back guarantee"
- Page 2: "90-day, no-questions-asked guarantee: You only pay if it works"
- **MERGED:** "90-day, no-questions-asked guarantee: You only pay if it works"

**When to merge:**
- Same core benefit/claim but different wording
- Similar pricing tiers (keep most complete version)
- Overlapping angles (combine into one stronger angle)
- Redundant hooks (keep the one with strongest emotional trigger)

---

**OUTPUT FORMAT:**

=== USPs ===
- [Item 1]
- [Item 2]
...

=== PRICING ===
- [Item 1]
- [Item 2]
...

=== MAIN ANGLE ===
- [Item 1]
- [Item 2]
...

=== TONE OF VOICE ===
- [Tone] - Example: "[quote]"
- [Tone] - Example: "[quote]"
...

=== KEY HOOKS ===
- [Hook 1]
- [Hook 2]
...

---

Return ONLY the consolidated list. No preamble, no commentary.
Start with "=== USPs ===" and end after the last hook.

options.systemMessage:
=You are a native advertising strategist curating the best marketing insights from multiple landing page analyses.

Your task: consolidate insights from multiple reference pages into a single, high-quality set of building blocks.

**Your job:**
1. Select the most effective items from all pages
2. Merge duplicate or similar items (keep best-worded version)
3. Prioritize specific, quantified, actionable items
4. Output up to the requested maximum per category

**Quality criteria:**
- Specific and concrete (with numbers/timeframes)
- Unique and differentiated
- Clear and direct

Output a curated, final list ready for user selection.

parameters.text:
=Extract reusable marketing elements from this native advertising landing page across 5 standardized categories.
These elements will be presented to users as selectable options when building new advertorials. Write each item as a direct statement or claim that can be reused, not as an analytical observation.

INPUT SOURCES:
- MARKDOWN (cleaned text content below)
- SCREENSHOT (provided as image)

---

USER CONTEXT (if provided):
{{ $('> 1 URL?').item.json.body.urls[0].description }}

If the user has provided context above, use it to:
- Understand what type of page this is (product page, competitor advertorial, landing page, etc.)
- Focus extraction on the areas they've highlighted as important
- Prioritize elements relevant to their stated goals
- Ignore sections they've indicated are not relevant

If no context is provided, extract comprehensively across all categories.

---

MARKDOWN CONTENT:
{{ $json.output }}

---

SCREENSHOT URL:
{{ $('Scrape (1)').item.json.data.screenshot }}

---

EXTRACTION REQUIREMENTS:

Extract elements for these 5 categories:

**1. USPs (Unique Selling Propositions)**
Extract 4-6 benefit statements, value propositions, or selling points exactly as they appear. These should be:
- Direct benefit claims (e.g., "Plans from just £7.50 per month")
- Specific guarantees or promises
- Social proof statements (e.g., "Trusted by 500,000+ families")
- Trust indicators (e.g., "No health questionnaires required")
- Risk reversals (e.g., "Money-back guarantee within 30 days")

Write each as a standalone claim that could be used in any advertorial.

**2. PRICING**
Extract pricing statements exactly as presented. Include:
- Price points with full context (e.g., "Standard Plan £12.99/month (was £19.99)")
- Payment options or tiers
- Discount offers
- Pricing qualifiers (e.g., "introductory rate", "covers up to £500/year")

If no pricing found, write: "No pricing information found"

**3. MAIN ANGLE (Messaging/Positioning)**
Extract 2-4 messaging approaches as directive statements:
- Target audience positioning (e.g., "Appeals to UK adults 30-60 worried about dental costs")
- Problem-solution framing (e.g., "Addresses NHS dentist shortage by offering immediate private coverage")
- Emotional triggers (e.g., "Creates urgency around avoiding unexpected emergency bills")
- Unique positioning (e.g., "Positions as affordable alternative to expensive private dentistry")

Write each as a messaging directive, not an observation.

**4. TONE OF VOICE**
Extract 2-3 tone characteristics as style directives with examples:
- Writing style to emulate
- Emotional approach
- Language patterns to use

Format: "[Tone directive] - Example: '[exact quote showing this tone]'"

**5. KEY HOOKS (Headlines & CTAs to Adapt)**
Extract 3-5 hooks as templates or examples:
- Headlines that work
- Subheadlines
- Opening teasers
- CTA phrasing
- Curiosity drivers

Format: "[Hook type]: '[exact text]'"

---

INSTRUCTIONS:
- Write as if suggesting "Include this" or "Use this approach" - NOT "This page does X"
- Extract exact text for USPs, pricing, and hooks
- Make tone and angle items actionable directives
- Each item should stand alone - no analytical framing
- Prioritize SCREENSHOT for headlines, prices, visual CTAs
- Prioritize MARKDOWN for body copy, benefits, details
- If user context emphasizes specific areas, weight extraction toward those areas

---

OUTPUT FORMAT:

=== USPs ===
- [Direct benefit claim or selling point]
- [Another benefit claim]
- [Continue for all USPs]

=== PRICING ===
- [Pricing statement with full context]
- [Another pricing statement]
- [Continue for all pricing found]

=== MAIN ANGLE ===
- [Messaging approach directive]
- [Another angle directive]
- [Continue for all angles]

=== TONE OF VOICE ===
- [Tone to emulate] - Example: "[exact quote demonstrating this]"
- [Another tone directive] - Example: "[exact quote]"
- [Continue for all tone characteristics]

=== KEY HOOKS ===
- [Hook type]: "[exact text to adapt or emulate]"
- [Hook type]: "[exact text]"
- [Continue for all hooks]

Do not add any preamble, commentary, or closing remarks. Start directly with "=== USPs ===" and end after the last hook.

options.systemMessage:
=You are a native advertising copywriting analyst extracting reusable marketing elements from high-performing landing pages.

You will receive:
1. Cleaned markdown content (text-based)
2. Screenshot of the page (visual-based)

Your task is to extract marketing elements as direct, reusable statements that can be selected and applied to new advertorials. These are NOT analytical observations - they are building blocks.

Extract what's working on this page in a format that can be directly reused. Write statements as if they are suggestions for what to include in a new campaign.

Cross-reference both sources. Visual elements take priority for headlines and CTAs.

Output your extraction in a clean, structured format with clear section headers.
